formula_note: Derived by triangulating explicit defender-fighter event deaths with mixed-status totals from hospitals and a medical committee. This estimate covers anti-Qadhafi Misrata defenders only; loyalist attacker deaths are not recovered in the current source base.
uncertainty_note: High uncertainty because no named-victim fighter ledger was found and because some mixed-status deaths may belong in the unknown-status bucket rather than military.
quality_note: Usable only as a defender-side military-loss estimate, not as a full-theater combatant total.

formula_note: Civilian deaths are bracketed between an identified-minimum civilian count (85 by 20 April), an early Amnesty floor (>100 by 21 March), and a majority-civilian morgue formulation (>267 bodies by 15 April, majority civilians), with additional April-May shelling retained in the upper scenarios.
uncertainty_note: Inflicting side is left null for the aggregate estimate because some sources describe civilian harm without a full attacker attribution for every death, even though most documented siege violence was by Qadhafi-aligned forces.
quality_note: Civilian estimate is auditable but still rests on mixed hospital/morgue sources rather than a complete named-victim roll.

formula_note: Unknown-status deaths are the remainder left deliberately unallocated after constructing civilian and defender-combatant ranges from mixed totals.
uncertainty_note: This bucket is substantial because available reporting often mixed civilians and fighters or reported only partial status identification.
quality_note: Necessary to avoid silently forcing mixed-status deaths into civilian or military categories.

formula_note: Piecewise-linear interpolation of McQuinn Figure 4 chartpoints from 2011-03-06 to 2011-05-15. Best estimate is approximately 4,800-5,000 fighter-months; low/high widen the chart-reading uncertainty.
uncertainty_note: This denominator excludes loyalist attacker exposure because no weekly attacker-strength series was found.
quality_note: Best available fighter exposure series for any Libya city episode in this pass, but still based on chart reading rather than raw tabular data.

formula_note: Population-present scenarios of 300,000 / 350,000 / 400,000 multiplied by 70 days of siege exposure (70 / 30.4375 months). Civilians are assumed to remain exposed if internally displaced within the city.
uncertainty_note: The main uncertainty is whether the operative city population was closer to the 300,000 humanitarian baseline or the 400,000 higher media/IOM figure; maritime evacuation appears too limited to justify a much lower stock.
quality_note: A defensible city-level exposure denominator, but still scenario-based rather than census-plus-daily-mobility derived.

uncertainty_note: This is a defender-side Misrata approximation, not a whole-theater core DR, because the military numerator and denominator cover anti-Qadhafi Misrata defenders only and exclude loyalist attacker exposure. Unknown-status deaths are left outside the ratio under a strict rule.
quality_note: Informative for within-city defender-versus-civilian lethality, but not valid as a full all-combatant DR for the entire battle.

formula_note: Direct civilian casualties from explosive violence only, as recorded by AOAV's EVMP.
uncertainty_note: Casualties include injuries as well as deaths and are mechanism-specific rather than all-violence.
quality_note: Auxiliary only; not part of core DR.
